title: Specklinia smaragdina
taxonomy: Specklinia smaragdina belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Pleurothallidinae. It is a species of orchid. Within the Orchidaceae family, Specklinia smaragdina is further classified as a genus within the tribe Epidendreae. The tribe Epidendreae is part of the subfamily Pleurothallidinae, which is a subfamily within the family Orchidaceae. Specklinia smaragdina's taxonomic classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, subfamily Pleurothallidinae, tribe Epidendreae, and genus Specklinia.
uses: Specklinia smaragdina is a species of orchid, and while its primary uses are largely ornamental and ecological, there is limited information available on its medicinal properties. However, some species within the Specklinia genus have been reported to have potential medicinal uses due to their bioactive compounds.
